name: Gradio
what_it_does: Python library for building interactive ML and AI demos with a simple API. Gradio lets data scientists and ML engineers create web UIs for models in a few lines of Python — file uploads, text boxes, image displays, chatbots. Every Gradio app automatically generates an HTTP API endpoint, making demos callable programmatically. Hugging Face Spaces uses Gradio as the primary demo format. Widely used for sharing models and building quick LLM interfaces.
use_cases: Rapidly prototype agent interfaces with chat UI — gr.ChatInterface creates a full conversational UI with streaming responses in ~10 lines
Expose agent pipelines as HTTP APIs automatically — every Gradio app's /predict endpoint enables agent-to-agent programmatic calls
Build shareable ML demos on Hugging Face Spaces that agents can also call via the auto-generated API
Create agent testing UIs with file upload, parameter sliders, and output visualization without frontend code
Chain multiple Gradio apps together using the programmatic client API — call another team's Gradio model endpoint from your agent
not_for: Production customer-facing applications — Gradio is optimized for demos and internal tools; use React/Next.js for production UX
High-concurrency applications — Gradio is single-threaded by default; scaling requires separate queue configuration
Complex multi-page applications with authentication — Gradio's auth is basic; use full web frameworks for complex apps
best_when: Rapidly building agent demos, ML model UIs, and programmatic API endpoints for AI models — especially when targeting Hugging Face Spaces sharing or internal team tooling.
avoid_when: Building production customer-facing UIs or complex multi-user applications — Gradio's simplicity is a strength for demos but a limitation for production apps.

auth.notes: No auth by default for local demos. Basic username/password auth via launch(auth=...) parameter. Hugging Face Spaces apps use HF token for private space access. The Python client uses hf_token for accessing private spaces.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully free (Apache 2.0). Hugging Face Spaces hosting has free CPU tier.
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid Gradio tiers. Hugging Face Spaces has GPU tiers ($0.05-$0.60/hour).
pricing.notes: Gradio library is completely free. Hosting on Hugging Face Spaces is free for CPU demos. GPU-backed demos require HF paid compute. Self-hosting is fully free.

performance.rate_limits: Default: 1 request at a time per app. Queue enables concurrent requests but adds latency. HF Spaces rate limits vary by hardware tier.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Auto-generated API has OpenAPI docs at /info endpoint. Python client provides typed errors. API responses are structured JSON. Streaming responses use SSE (Server-Sent Events). Error handling is good for a demo-focused framework.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: REST API endpoints are stateless — same inputs produce same outputs (assuming deterministic models). Session state is optional and app-specific. Idempotent by default.
known_agent_gotchas: Gradio's queue must be enabled (demo.queue()) for concurrent requests — without queue, simultaneous API calls return 503 errors
The programmatic API endpoint URL pattern changed between Gradio versions — agents should check the /info endpoint for current API documentation
File uploads via Gradio API require base64 encoding or multipart form data — not plain JSON; handle accordingly
Gradio's streaming interface uses SSE (Server-Sent Events) — agents must handle SSE stream parsing, not standard REST JSON responses
Hugging Face Spaces apps may sleep after inactivity — first request after sleep has cold start latency (10-60 seconds)
Gradio app's session state (gr.State) is per-user session, not globally shared — agents calling the API don't share state with browser users

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source. HTTPS on HF Spaces. Basic auth is password-only (no MFA, no tokens) — not suitable for sensitive production apps. Demo-focused security model. Self-hosted deployments must add their own auth layer.
